text:
Once upon a time in the little village of Pinehill, there lived a shy squirrel named Oliver. He was tiny and had a big heart. Every day Oliver would amble through the woodlands gathering seeds and acorns. One particular day, he heard a soft whimpering.

As he moved closer, Oliver discovered a small, lonely rabbitskin scarf. It belonged to a wise old owl who lived alone atop the tallest oak. Overwhelmed by kindness, Oliver took the scarf. The owl didn't say much; just a grateful "Thank you."

Every evening from that tree, the owl told stories of old times. Stories about wise warriors, kind hearted cats, and the ancient forest's secret history.

One night, the owl whispered a bedtime tale. "There's a treasure hidden somewhere in this wood. A gold star."

Oliver pondered this mystery for a moonlit night. Come morning, he'd searched high and low. Nothing. He decided to make a wish. "
